author: bharath.vedartham
content:
I have a question w.r.t proposer preferences. How would a validator client like vouch communicate the proposer preferences to the beacon node?
I think beacon nodes would implement the `/eth/v1/validator/proposer_preferences` defined at: https://github.com/ethereum/beacon-APIs/pull/608/changes#diff-faf0bd13f84da9cddead43dc8482896e52d1d19db09a2d688b6fa18f977ec003R1 and vouch would have to call this API to the beacon node with the proposer preferences they create right?

author: nflaig
content: yes they would submit via that api, I don't see how vouch is different than any other vc here

author: nflaig
content: <@884836500037066862> prysm already handles this? and <@1035747431461167125> can  you add some rational to your PR please

author: bharath.vedartham
content: yes that makes sense, I just wanted to confirm whether this was indeed the right beacon-api they would have to use

author: nflaig
content: isn't it better to just use the parent gas limit as fallback, ie. not change the gas limit at all?

author: nflaig
content: I think we should just have a single api that returns the `Builder` spec object, left more comments on the PR

author: nflaig
content: that works but definitely not great, I don't want to query the full state either just to get builder info, we should introduce a minimal api to not have to do this

author: bharath.vedartham
content: yeah i m happy with the APIs that potuz recommended. If clients implemented them, I would def use that instead of querying the full state

author: potuz
content: It's absolutely fine guys. I don't care which API y'all prefer but for the love of God can you agree on one and merge it? Otherwise I'll just implement the above in Prysm and use that for the builder

author: nflaig
content: I don't like any of the endpoints in your PR 😅 , the closest is probably `postStateBuilders` but we should not return a custom object in my opinion, so if you wanna implement one now I would just do that and return `List[Builder]` in the response, and as Paul mentioned here https://discord.com/channels/595666850260713488/710831784991916072/1508589845784690809, we likely just wanna use `POST` to allow passing more than >64 pubkeys in the filter

author: nflaig
content: well prysm right now is the only client that started to implement this, what kind of api do you need right now? I think we can just add the minimal required api for now, and discuss others later if needed

author: nflaig
content: the simplest would be `GET /eth/v1/beacon/states/{state_id}/builders` to return all builders always, but I suspect we wanna have some filtering before returning the response, but for example do we even need to filter by `status`?

author: potuz
content: I need what I wrote, so if you guys want to change it I'm happy as long as I keep the same functionality at least.

author: potuz
content: I definitely do not want to have an endpoint that returns all builders

author: potuz
content: cause I want to query for my own builder only

author: potuz
content: I don' t want to have to deal with possibly thousands of keys in return and have to parse them

author: potuz
content: The crucial thing here is to query by pubkey to get the status and the builder index

author: potuz
content: which is the minimum my PR achieves

author: potuz
content: and balance of course

author: nflaig
content: yes, so minimal api seems to be one that allows to filter by pubkey (or index) and return one or more builders, though I would just return `Builder` object as in the spec, you should be able to figure out the status on the client side?

author: potuz
content: I would prefer to have the beacon do stateful computations or have to deal with the spec, the client does not need to know it

author: potuz
content: but then again you are achieving making me involved in the already crowded API kitchen

author: jameshe5018
content: maybe we should do that, right now i have it falling back to local default

author: jameshe5018
content: need to double check. we don't have it hooked up yet

author: jameshe5018
content: for rest at least

author: jameshe5018
content: can you add that as feedback on my open pr btw?

author: jameshe5018
content: 404 sounds good could do that

author: nflaig
content: I would prefer to have the beacon do
